title: phacellanthus tubiflorus
intro: Phacellanthus tubiflorus, also known as the Gold Mold or False Gold, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern regions of the United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Phacellanthus tubiflorus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to support respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be used topically in salves or creams.
